body:
Three upgrades to implement:

1. WATCHDOG: Wire up the agent-resilience skill properly. Every external tool call (compile, git push, network) must be wrapped with a hard timeout (120s compile, 30s git, 60s network). Write a heartbeat to $HOME/.relay-mailbox/.heartbeat before each operation: {ts, op, status:running}. Update to status:done on completion. If a watchdog check finds status:running with ts older than 15 min, treat as stalled -- resume from .checkpoint if present, else report stall via relay. Create a .checkpoint file at the start of every multi-step task with the current step index.

2. FILE PASSING (bidirectional): The relay repo now has a files/ directory alongside messages/. Structure: files/from-chat/ (I send files to you), files/from-agent/ (you send files to me), files/_archive/ (processed). Protocol:
- To SEND a file to me: copy it into files/from-agent/{ts}_{filename}, commit, push. I will pull and read it.
- To RECEIVE a file from me: pull files/from-chat/ at start of every turn alongside the message check. Process and archive same as messages.
- Files can be any type: .mq4, .md, .txt, .json, .log -- commit them as-is, no encoding needed.
- Add a companion .json manifest alongside each file: {id, timestamp, direction, filename, description, requires_consent}.

3. PRACTICAL BACK AND FORTH: Update relay-to-chat skill so that after completing any significant task, TCA proactively sends: (a) a summary message, (b) any output files (compiled EA, logs, diffs) into files/from-agent/ automatically -- no explicit instruction needed. I will pull and report back to Manuel directly.

Update all source scripts and skills. Confirm working and report back.
